articles.headline: Nation Observes Gandhi Jayanti, Swachh Bharat Mission Continues Its Transformative Journey
articles.explainer:
• India commemorates the 156th birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi, Father of the Nation, with renewed pledges to his ideals of truth, non-violence, and cleanliness.
• Prime Minister leads tributes at Rajghat, emphasizing the contemporary relevance of Gandhian philosophy in nation-building.
• The Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM), launched on this day in 2014, celebrates over a decade of progress in sanitation and waste management.
• Focus shifts to SBM-Grameen Phase II and SBM-Urban 2.0, aiming for sustained behavioral change and comprehensive waste management.
• Discussions highlight the intersection of cleanliness with public health, environmental sustainability, and economic development.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Gandhi Jayanti is observed on October 2nd, marking Mahatma Gandhi's birth anniversary.
Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM) was launched on October 2, 2014.
SBM has two sub-missions: SBM (Gramin) and SBM (Urban).
SBM-Grameen Phase II focuses on sustaining ODF status and solid/liquid waste management.
SBM-Urban 2.0 aims for 'Garbage Free Cities'.
International Day of Non-Violence is also observed on October 2nd.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and impact of the Swachh Bharat Mission on public health and environmental sustainability in India.
Analyze the relevance of Gandhian principles like 'Gram Swaraj' and 'Trusteeship' in contemporary rural development and economic policy.
Evaluate how the celebration of Gandhi Jayanti reinforces national values and promotes social cohesion in India.
Examine the challenges and successes of achieving universal sanitation coverage under SBM and future strategies for waste management.
articles.imageDescription: A commemorative stamp featuring Mahatma Gandhi, with the Swachh Bharat Mission logo in the background.
articles.trivia: Mahatma Gandhi's actual birth year was 1869, making October 2, 2025, his 156th birth anniversary.

articles.headline: Nation Remembers Lal Bahadur Shastri on His 121st Birth Anniversary
articles.explainer:
• India pays homage to its second Prime Minister, Lal Bahadur Shastri, on his 121st birth anniversary, celebrated alongside Gandhi Jayanti.
• Leaders across the political spectrum highlight Shastri's simplicity, integrity, and decisive leadership during challenging times.
• His iconic slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' continues to resonate, emphasizing the critical role of India's soldiers and farmers.
• Discussions focus on his contributions to the Green Revolution and strengthening India's defense capabilities.
• Government initiatives in agriculture and national security are often linked to Shastri's vision.
gsSubTopics: Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country.
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: Lal Bahadur Shastri's birth anniversary is on October 2nd.
He was India's second Prime Minister.
His famous slogan was 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan'.
He played a key role during the 1965 Indo-Pak War.
He signed the Tashkent Declaration.
He promoted the Green Revolution in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of Lal Bahadur Shastri's leadership during the 1965 Indo-Pak War and its impact on India's strategic posture.
Discuss the long-term implications of the 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' slogan on India's agricultural and defense policies.
Evaluate Shastri's contribution to India's food security and the initiation of the Green Revolution.
Examine how Shastri's principles of simplicity and integrity continue to inspire public service in India.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of Lal Bahadur Shastri, with the Indian Parliament in the background.
articles.trivia: Lal Bahadur Shastri was the first person to be posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award, in 1966.

articles.headline: International Day of Non-Violence Emphasizes Global Peace and Tolerance
articles.explainer:
• The United Nations observes the International Day of Non-Violence on October 2nd, coinciding with Mahatma Gandhi's birth anniversary.
• UN Secretary-General issues a statement calling for renewed commitment to non-violence in resolving conflicts and promoting human rights.
• Global leaders and civil society organizations hold events to discuss the relevance of non-violent resistance in contemporary challenges.
• Focus on addressing hate speech, discrimination, and conflicts through peaceful means and dialogue.
• The day serves as a reminder of Gandhi's philosophy as a tool for social change and international peace.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: International Day of Non-Violence is observed annually on October 2nd.
It was established by the UN General Assembly in 2007.
The day promotes non-violence through education and public awareness.
It coincides with the birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of non-violent movements in achieving political and social change globally, with specific examples.
Analyze how Mahatma Gandhi's philosophy of non-violence remains relevant in addressing contemporary global challenges like terrorism and climate change.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international organizations like the UN in promoting peace and non-violence amidst geopolitical tensions.
Examine the challenges in implementing non-violent approaches to conflict resolution in an increasingly complex world.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations logo with a dove symbolizing peace.
articles.trivia: The resolution to establish the International Day of Non-Violence was co-sponsored by 140 countries, reflecting its broad international support.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivers a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The Court clarifies provisions related to cross-border data transfers and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• Petitions challenging the Act on grounds of privacy infringement and excessive government access are partially dismissed.
• The ruling emphasizes the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests in data processing.
• This verdict provides crucial legal clarity for businesses and government agencies operating in the digital space.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was passed by Parliament in August 2023.
It aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
The Act recognizes the right to privacy as a fundamental right (Puttaswamy judgment).
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
The Act defines 'data fiduciary' and 'data principal'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its implications for individual privacy and data governance in India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by the DPDP Act for businesses operating in the digital economy.
Evaluate the Supreme Court's role in balancing fundamental rights with state interests in the context of data protection.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how the Indian DPDP Act aligns or diverges from them.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, symbolizing a court judgment.
articles.trivia: The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in 2017 in the K.S. Puttaswamy case.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties and Domestic Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concludes its bi-monthly review, opting to keep the repo rate unchanged.
• Decision cited global economic slowdown, geopolitical tensions, and persistent domestic food inflation as key factors.
• RBI Governor reiterates commitment to inflation targeting while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future rate actions contingent on inflation trajectory and growth momentum.
• The move aims to provide stability to financial markets and anchor inflation expectations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy and fiscal policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body (3 RBI, 3 GoI nominees).
The MPC determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target for India is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global and domestic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
Evaluate the impact of global geopolitical events and supply chain disruptions on India's inflation trajectory and RBI's policy decisions.
Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in ensuring transparency and accountability in India's monetary policy framework.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Climate Targets, Highlights Progress in Renewable Energy
articles.explainer:
• On the sidelines of global climate discussions, India reiterates its ambitious Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change releases a report detailing advancements in renewable energy capacity, particularly solar and wind power.
• Challenges remain in financing adaptation measures and transitioning away from fossil fuels.
• India calls for greater climate finance and technology transfer from developed nations.
• Focus on sustainable development goals and green hydrogen initiatives to meet future energy demands.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India updated its NDCs in 2022, committing to reduce emissions intensity by 45% by 2030 from 2005 levels.
India aims for about 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030.
The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India.
Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2 degrees Celsius.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's progress and challenges in achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
Discuss the potential of renewable energy, particularly green hydrogen, in decarbonizing India's economy and ensuring energy security.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and climate finance in enabling developing countries like India to meet their climate goals.
Examine the environmental and socio-economic impacts of India's transition towards a low-carbon economy.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels generating electricity in a field, symbolizing renewable energy.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases globally, but its per capita emissions are significantly lower than many developed nations.

articles.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Chandrayaan-4 Mission Preparations for Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) releases a statement on the advanced stages of preparation for the Chandrayaan-4 mission.
• The mission aims for a lunar sample return, building on the success of Chandrayaan-3's soft landing.
• Focus on developing advanced robotic systems and sample collection mechanisms for autonomous operation on the lunar surface.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a crucial step towards future human spaceflight missions and deeper lunar exploration.
• The mission highlights India's growing capabilities in complex interplanetary missions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-1 was India's first lunar probe (2008).
Chandrayaan-2 attempted a soft landing (2019) but the lander crashed.
Chandrayaan-3 successfully soft-landed a lander (Vikram) and rover (Pragyan) on the lunar south pole in August 2023.
Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a lunar sample return mission.
ISRO is the national space agency of India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's lunar missions (Chandrayaan series) for scientific research and technological advancement.
Analyze the challenges involved in a lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the role of India's space program in fostering scientific temper and inspiring innovation among youth.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its potential for international collaboration.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Chandrayaan-4 spacecraft orbiting the Moon.
articles.trivia: India became the fourth country to achieve a soft landing on the Moon and the first to land near the lunar south pole with Chandrayaan-3.

articles.headline: India and Japan Hold Annual Bilateral Summit, Focus on Economic & Strategic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister of India hosts his Japanese counterpart for the annual bilateral summit, reinforcing the 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
• Discussions center on enhancing economic ties, including investment, trade, and infrastructure development.
• Both leaders emphasize cooperation in defense, security, and clean energy technologies.
• Regional security issues, particularly in the Indo-Pacific, feature prominently in the talks.
• Joint statements highlight commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and collaboration in multilateral forums.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan hold annual bilateral summits.
Their relationship is designated as a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
Both countries are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue).
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects (e.g., High-Speed Rail).
Cooperation extends to defense, nuclear energy, and space.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of India-Japan relations into a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' and its significance for regional stability.
Discuss the key areas of cooperation between India and Japan, particularly in economic development, defense, and the Indo-Pacific strategy.
Evaluate the role of India and Japan within multilateral groupings like the Quad in addressing contemporary geopolitical challenges.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for further deepening bilateral ties, including trade imbalances and technological collaboration.
articles.imageDescription: The Prime Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands, with their national flags in the background.
articles.trivia: Japan is a major contributor to India's infrastructure development, notably through its Official Development Assistance (ODA), including for the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail Project.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Comprehensive Report on Sustainable Rural Development Goals
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog publishes its annual report assessing India's progress towards achieving Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in rural areas.
• The report highlights successes in poverty reduction, access to clean water, and rural electrification.
• Identifies persistent challenges in quality education, healthcare infrastructure, and gender equality in remote regions.
• Recommends policy interventions focusing on localized planning, community participation, and digital inclusion.
• The findings will inform future government strategies for holistic rural development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
keyTerms: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.
It replaced the Planning Commission in 2015.
NITI Aayog prepares long-term policy and program frameworks.
The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a collection of 17 global goals set by the United Nations.
India is a signatory to the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of NITI Aayog in shaping India's rural development policies and monitoring progress on Sustainable Development Goals.
Discuss the major achievements and persistent challenges in achieving inclusive and sustainable rural development in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes aimed at poverty reduction, improving rural infrastructure, and enhancing access to basic services.
Examine the importance of localized planning and community participation in ensuring the success of rural development initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representing the Sustainable Development Goals with rural Indian landscapes.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog's full form is National Institution for Transforming India.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Vocational Training and Multidisciplinary Learning in 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announces new directives for the phased implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in higher education.
• Emphasis on integrating vocational training and skill development into mainstream curricula across universities and colleges.
• Push for multidisciplinary and holistic education, breaking down rigid disciplinary silos.
• Initiatives to enhance faculty training and adopt technology-driven learning platforms.
• The goal is to produce a workforce equipped with 21st-century skills and foster critical thinking.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the NEP of 1986.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Key features include 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, multidisciplinary approach, vocational integration.
The Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) allows credit transfer.
NEP 2020 aims to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in reshaping India's education landscape.
Discuss the challenges in implementing vocational training and multidisciplinary learning across diverse educational institutions in India.
Evaluate how NEP 2020 aims to address issues of quality, access, and equity in higher education.
Examine the role of technology and faculty development in achieving the goals of the National Education Policy.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in a vocational training workshop, symbolizing skill development.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Digital Health Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) releases a progress report on the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), highlighting its expanding reach.
• Over 500 million health IDs (ABHA IDs) registered, facilitating digital health records and seamless access to healthcare services.
• Focus on integrating private healthcare providers and expanding the network of Health Facility Registry (HFR) and Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR).
• Challenges remain in ensuring digital literacy and privacy concerns in remote and underserved areas.
• ABDM aims to create a unified digital health infrastructure for efficient and equitable healthcare delivery.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) was launched in September 2021.
It aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country.
Key components include Health ID (ABHA), Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR), and Health Facility Registry (HFR).
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
ABDM facilitates digital access to health records and telemedicine services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to transform India's healthcare delivery system.
Discuss the challenges in ensuring data privacy, digital literacy, and equitable access to digital health services across India.
Evaluate the role of technology and public-private partnerships in expanding the reach and effectiveness of ABDM.
Examine how ABDM aligns with India's broader vision of 'Digital India' and universal healthcare coverage.
articles.imageDescription: A digital graphic depicting interconnected healthcare services and an ABHA ID card.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Indigenous Defense Manufacturing with New Policy Incentives
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Defence announces a fresh set of policy reforms and incentives to promote 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defense production.
• Focus on encouraging private sector participation, MSMEs, and startups in developing cutting-edge defense technologies.
• Measures include streamlined procurement procedures, increased budget allocation for domestic R&D, and technology transfer facilitation.
• The goal is to reduce import dependency and position India as a global defense manufacturing hub.
• This move is crucial for national security and economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan includes a focus on self-reliance in defense production.
The Defense Acquisition Procedure (DAP) promotes 'Make in India'.
India aims to become a net exporter of defense equipment.
Defense Industrial Corridors have been established in Uttar Pradesh and Tamil Nadu.
Positive Indigenisation Lists restrict import of certain defense items.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defense manufacturing for India's national security and economic development.
Discuss the policy incentives and reforms introduced by the government to boost indigenous defense production.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the private sector, MSMEs, and startups in contributing to India's defense industrial base.
Examine the potential of India to emerge as a global hub for defense manufacturing and exports.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian-made fighter jet taking off, symbolizing indigenous defense capabilities.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defense equipment, but efforts are underway to reverse this trend through indigenization.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India announce the discovery of a previously unknown species of frog in the Western Ghats.
• The discovery highlights the immense biodiversity and ecological fragility of the region.
• The new species is characterized by unique morphological features and genetic markers.
• Calls for enhanced conservation efforts and protection of critical habitats in the Western Ghats.
• This finding underscores the importance of continued scientific exploration and biodiversity mapping.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
It is home to a large number of endemic species.
Amphibians are bio-indicators of environmental health.
The Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) is India's premier organization for zoological research.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for understanding ecosystem health and guiding conservation strategies.
Evaluate the challenges in protecting endemic species in regions prone to habitat degradation and climate change.
Examine the role of scientific institutions like ZSI in biodiversity research and conservation efforts in India.
articles.imageDescription: A newly discovered frog species camouflaged in the lush foliage of the Western Ghats.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and influence the Indian monsoon weather patterns.

articles.headline: India Pushes for G20 Consensus on Sustainable Infrastructure Financing
articles.explainer:
• India, as a key player in the G20, actively advocates for stronger international cooperation on sustainable infrastructure financing.
• Discussions at a G20 working group meeting emphasize innovative financing mechanisms and private sector participation.
• Focus on green infrastructure projects, climate-resilient development, and digital connectivity in developing nations.
• India highlights its own experience with large-scale infrastructure development and public-private partnerships.
• The initiative aims to bridge the global infrastructure gap and promote inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
It addresses major issues related to the global economy.
India held the G20 presidency in 2023.
Sustainable infrastructure is a key focus area for global development.
Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) play a crucial role in infrastructure financing.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and objectives in advocating for sustainable infrastructure financing within the G20 framework.
Discuss the importance of green infrastructure and climate-resilient development for achieving global sustainable development goals.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in mobilizing private sector investment for large-scale infrastructure projects in developing countries.
Examine how innovative financing mechanisms can help bridge the global infrastructure gap and promote inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green elements, symbolizing sustainable infrastructure.
articles.trivia: The G20 accounts for around 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

articles.headline: Government Expands Micro-Irrigation Scheme to Boost Water Use Efficiency in Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announces a significant expansion of the 'Per Drop More Crop' component of the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY).
• Increased subsidies and technical assistance for farmers adopting drip and sprinkler irrigation systems.
• Focus on water-stressed regions to enhance agricultural productivity and conserve water resources.
• Awareness campaigns launched to educate farmers on the long-term benefits of micro-irrigation.
• This initiative is crucial for achieving sustainable agriculture and improving farmer incomes.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) was launched in 2015.
Its motto is 'Har Khet Ko Paani' (Water to Every Field).
'Per Drop More Crop' is a component of PMKSY, focusing on micro-irrigation.
Micro-irrigation techniques include drip and sprinkler irrigation.
These methods significantly improve water use efficiency in agriculture.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of micro-irrigation in addressing water scarcity and enhancing agricultural productivity in India.
Discuss the challenges in the widespread adoption of drip and sprinkler irrigation systems by Indian farmers.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes like PMKSY in promoting water conservation and improving farmer incomes.
Examine the potential of technological interventions and farmer awareness programs in achieving sustainable water management in agriculture.
articles.imageDescription: A field being irrigated using a drip irrigation system, showing efficient water use.
articles.trivia: India has the largest irrigated land area in the world, but traditional flood irrigation leads to significant water wastage.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework to Boost Women Entrepreneurship and Self-Help Groups
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Women and Child Development, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, launches a comprehensive policy aimed at fostering women entrepreneurship.
• The framework includes enhanced access to credit, mentorship programs, and market linkages for women-led businesses.
• Special focus on strengthening Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and integrating them into value chains.
• Initiatives to provide digital literacy and business management skills to women in rural and urban areas.
• The policy seeks to empower women economically and promote inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment.
prelimsPoints: Self-Help Groups (SHGs) are informal associations of people who choose to come together to find ways to improve their living conditions.
SHGs are crucial for financial inclusion and women's empowerment.
The Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) promotes SHGs.
Government schemes like 'Stand-Up India' support women entrepreneurs.
Gender Budgeting is a tool to assess the impact of government policies on women.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of women entrepreneurship and Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in promoting socio-economic development and gender equality in India.
Discuss the key challenges faced by women entrepreneurs in accessing finance, markets, and skills, and how the new policy addresses them.
Evaluate the role of government schemes and policy frameworks in fostering an enabling ecosystem for women-led businesses.
Examine the potential of digital literacy and technology adoption in empowering rural women entrepreneurs.
articles.imageDescription: A group of women entrepreneurs showcasing their products, symbolizing economic empowerment.
articles.trivia: India has over 12 million Self-Help Groups, with over 88% of them being all-women SHGs.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Shows Progress in River Rejuvenation and Public Participation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) releases an updated report on the progress of the Namami Gange programme.
• Highlights significant improvements in wastewater treatment infrastructure and ecological flow in stretches of the Ganges.
• Increased public awareness and community involvement in 'Ganga Praharis' and 'Ganga Mitras' initiatives.
• Challenges persist in industrial pollution control and maintaining the ecological integrity of tributaries.
• The mission aims for 'Aviral' (uninterrupted) and 'Nirmal' (clean) Ganga, critical for India's cultural and environmental heritage.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Programme was launched in 2014.
It is an integrated conservation mission to abate pollution and conserve and rejuvenate the National River Ganga.
The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) is the implementing agency.
Key pillars include sewage infrastructure, industrial pollution abatement, riverfront development, biodiversity conservation.
The Hybrid Annuity Model (HAM) is used for building STPs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted approach of the Namami Gange Programme in addressing pollution and rejuvenating the River Ganga.
Discuss the role of public participation and technological interventions in achieving the mission's objectives.
Evaluate the challenges faced in controlling industrial pollution and ensuring ecological flow in the Ganga basin.
Examine the cultural and ecological significance of the River Ganga and the importance of its sustained rejuvenation.
articles.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the River Ganga with ghats and temples in the background.
articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populated river basin in the world, home to over 500 million people.

articles.headline: Startup India Initiative Bolsters Innovation Ecosystem, Focus on Deep Tech and Rural Startups
articles.explainer:
• Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) releases a report on the growth of India's startup ecosystem under the 'Startup India' initiative.
• Highlights increasing number of recognized startups, significant venture capital investments, and job creation.
• New policy measures announced to support deep tech startups and promote entrepreneurship in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, and rural areas.
• Focus on intellectual property rights protection and ease of doing business for nascent ventures.
• India solidifies its position as the third-largest startup ecosystem globally.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Startup India initiative was launched in 2016.
It aims to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups in the country.
DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade) is the nodal agency.
India is the third-largest startup ecosystem globally (after the US and China).
Schemes like 'Fund of Funds for Startups' and 'Startup India Seed Fund Scheme' support startups.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of the Startup India initiative on fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for deep tech startups and those emerging from Tier 2/3 cities and rural areas.
Evaluate the role of government policies and financial support mechanisms in creating a conducive environment for startup growth.
Examine how the startup ecosystem contributes to job creation, economic growth, and technological advancements in India.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space.
articles.trivia: India added a record number of unicorns (startups valued over $1 billion) in recent years, reflecting the vibrancy of its ecosystem.

articles.headline: India-Central Asia Dialogue Focuses on Connectivity, Trade, and Regional Security
articles.explainer:
• The latest edition of the India-Central Asia Dialogue concludes with renewed pledges for enhanced cooperation across various sectors.
• Foreign Ministers discuss increasing trade volumes, establishing reliable connectivity corridors, and energy security.
• Emphasis on regional security challenges, including the situation in Afghanistan and countering terrorism.
• India reiterates its commitment to the 'Connect Central Asia' policy and strengthening historical and cultural ties.
• Joint statement calls for greater collaboration in multilateral forums and people-to-people exchanges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The India-Central Asia Dialogue is a ministerial-level forum.
It involves India and five Central Asian countries: Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan.
India's 'Connect Central Asia Policy' was launched in 2012.
Key areas of cooperation include trade, connectivity, energy, and security.
Chabahar Port in Iran is crucial for India's connectivity to Central Asia.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing connectivity and trade between India and Central Asian countries.
Evaluate India's role in addressing regional security concerns, particularly the situation in Afghanistan, through the India-Central Asia Dialogue.
Examine how historical and cultural linkages can be leveraged to strengthen people-to-people ties between India and Central Asia.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing trade routes connecting India to Central Asian countries.
articles.trivia: Central Asia is rich in natural resources, including oil, natural gas, and minerals, making it strategically important.

highlights: Nation celebrates 156th Gandhi Jayanti, renewing commitment to Swachh Bharat Mission and its goals.
Lal Bahadur Shastri's 121st birth anniversary observed, remembering his 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' legacy.
Supreme Court upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Act, bringing clarity to India's data privacy regime.
RBI maintains status quo on repo rate amidst global economic uncertainties and domestic inflation concerns.
ISRO provides update on Chandrayaan-4, gearing up for a complex lunar sample return mission.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Swachh Bharat Mission', consider the following statements:
1. It was launched on October 2, 2014.
2. Its primary goal is to achieve 'Open Defecation Free' (ODF) status across rural and urban India.
3. The Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) is implemented by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Swachh Bharat Mission was indeed launched on October 2, 2014, with the primary goal of achieving ODF status. SBM (Urban) is implemented by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, while SBM (Gramin) is under the Ministry of Jal Shakti.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following slogans is famously associated with former Indian Prime Minister Lal Bahadur Shastri?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' (Hail the Soldier, Hail the Farmer) was coined by Lal Bahadur Shastri during the Indo-Pak War of 1965 to boost the morale of soldiers and farmers. 'Garibi Hatao' was associated with Indira Gandhi, 'Quit India' was a call by Mahatma Gandhi, and 'Inquilab Zindabad' was coined by Maulana Hasrat Mohani and popularized by Bhagat Singh.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023:
1. It recognizes the right to privacy as a fundamental right.
2. It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
3. It applies to the processing of digital personal data outside India if it involves offering goods or services to data principals in India.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The DPDP Act, 2023, builds upon the Supreme Court's recognition of the right to privacy, establishes the Data Protection Board, and has an extra-territorial application covering processing activities related to offering goods or services to individuals in India.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is a six-member body, with three members from the Reserve Bank of India and three external members nominated by the Central Government.
2. The Governor of the Reserve Bank of India is the ex-officio Chairperson of the MPC.
3. The MPC determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All the statements are correct. The MPC indeed has six members (3 RBI, 3 GoI nominees), with the RBI Governor as the ex-officio Chairperson, and its mandate is to determine the policy rates to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a component or objective of the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: PMKSY (Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana) focuses on irrigation and water use efficiency, with components like 'Har Khet Ko Paani' (aiming for assured irrigation) and 'Per Drop More Crop' (promoting micro-irrigation) and also incorporates AIBP for major/medium irrigation projects. Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) is a crop insurance scheme, separate from PMKSY, although both are agricultural initiatives.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the multifaceted impact of the Swachh Bharat Mission on public health, environmental sustainability, and behavioral change in India. What are the key challenges that need to be addressed for its sustained success in Phase 2?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Social empowerment; GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; GS3: Environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce SBM and its objectives. Discuss its impact on public health (reduced diseases), environment (waste management), and society (behavioral change, dignity). Highlight successes like ODF status. Then, detail challenges for Phase 2, such as sustaining ODF, managing solid/liquid waste, behavioral inertia, financial constraints, and technological adoption. Conclude with recommendations for holistic implementation.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the significance of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in safeguarding individual privacy and regulating data governance in India. Critically evaluate its provisions concerning data fiduciaries, data principal rights, and cross-border data transfers.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights; Government policies and interventions; GS3: Science and Technology- IT and Computers.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by contextualizing the Act with the right to privacy (Puttaswamy judgment). Explain its objectives and key definitions (data fiduciary, data principal). Analyze provisions related to consent, data protection obligations, and the Data Protection Board. Critically evaluate the balance between state access and individual rights, the framework for cross-border data flow, and potential implementation challenges for businesses and individuals. Conclude on its role in India's digital economy.

mainsMocks.question: India's space program has achieved remarkable feats with missions like Chandrayaan-3. Discuss the scientific and strategic implications of ISRO's future lunar missions, such as Chandrayaan-4, focusing on lunar sample return. How do these missions contribute to India's technological prowess and global standing?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications; Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's space program and the success of Chandrayaan-3. Elaborate on the scientific objectives of a lunar sample return mission (e.g., understanding lunar geology, origin of solar system). Discuss the technological challenges (precision landing, robotic arm, sample collection, return capsule). Explain strategic implications (global leadership, dual-use technologies, international collaboration). Conclude on the impact on India's scientific temper, economic growth, and geopolitical influence.

quickBytes.text: Gandhi Jayanti is one of the three national holidays in India, observed on October 2nd.
Lal Bahadur Shastri served as India's Prime Minister from 1964 to 1966.
The International Day of Non-Violence was established by the UN General Assembly in 2007.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is India's first comprehensive data protection law.
Repo rate is the interest rate at which the RBI provides liquidity to commercial banks.
India aims for 50% non-fossil fuel-based energy capacity by 2030, as per its NDCs.
ISRO's Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed near the Moon's South Pole in August 2023.
The Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) includes India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015.
The 'Per Drop More Crop' component promotes micro-irrigation under PMKSY.

GS1.headline: Nation Observes Gandhi Jayanti, Swachh Bharat Mission Continues Its Transformative Journey
GS1.explainer:
• India commemorates the 156th birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi, Father of the Nation, with renewed pledges to his ideals of truth, non-violence, and cleanliness.
• Prime Minister leads tributes at Rajghat, emphasizing the contemporary relevance of Gandhian philosophy in nation-building.
• The Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM), launched on this day in 2014, celebrates over a decade of progress in sanitation and waste management.
• Focus shifts to SBM-Grameen Phase II and SBM-Urban 2.0, aiming for sustained behavioral change and comprehensive waste management.
• Discussions highlight the intersection of cleanliness with public health, environmental sustainability, and economic development.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Gandhi Jayanti is observed on October 2nd, marking Mahatma Gandhi's birth anniversary.
Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM) was launched on October 2, 2014.
SBM has two sub-missions: SBM (Gramin) and SBM (Urban).
SBM-Grameen Phase II focuses on sustaining ODF status and solid/liquid waste management.
SBM-Urban 2.0 aims for 'Garbage Free Cities'.
International Day of Non-Violence is also observed on October 2nd.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and impact of the Swachh Bharat Mission on public health and environmental sustainability in India.
Analyze the relevance of Gandhian principles like 'Gram Swaraj' and 'Trusteeship' in contemporary rural development and economic policy.
Evaluate how the celebration of Gandhi Jayanti reinforces national values and promotes social cohesion in India.
Examine the challenges and successes of achieving universal sanitation coverage under SBM and future strategies for waste management.
GS1.imageDescription: A commemorative stamp featuring Mahatma Gandhi, with the Swachh Bharat Mission logo in the background.
GS1.trivia: Mahatma Gandhi's actual birth year was 1869, making October 2, 2025, his 156th birth anniversary.

GS1.headline: Nation Remembers Lal Bahadur Shastri on His 121st Birth Anniversary
GS1.explainer:
• India pays homage to its second Prime Minister, Lal Bahadur Shastri, on his 121st birth anniversary, celebrated alongside Gandhi Jayanti.
• Leaders across the political spectrum highlight Shastri's simplicity, integrity, and decisive leadership during challenging times.
• His iconic slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' continues to resonate, emphasizing the critical role of India's soldiers and farmers.
• Discussions focus on his contributions to the Green Revolution and strengthening India's defense capabilities.
• Government initiatives in agriculture and national security are often linked to Shastri's vision.
gsSubTopics: Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country.
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: Lal Bahadur Shastri's birth anniversary is on October 2nd.
He was India's second Prime Minister.
His famous slogan was 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan'.
He played a key role during the 1965 Indo-Pak War.
He signed the Tashkent Declaration.
He promoted the Green Revolution in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of Lal Bahadur Shastri's leadership during the 1965 Indo-Pak War and its impact on India's strategic posture.
Discuss the long-term implications of the 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' slogan on India's agricultural and defense policies.
Evaluate Shastri's contribution to India's food security and the initiation of the Green Revolution.
Examine how Shastri's principles of simplicity and integrity continue to inspire public service in India.
GS1.imageDescription: A portrait of Lal Bahadur Shastri, with the Indian Parliament in the background.
GS1.trivia: Lal Bahadur Shastri was the first person to be posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award, in 1966.

GS1.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework to Boost Women Entrepreneurship and Self-Help Groups
GS1.explainer:
• Ministry of Women and Child Development, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, launches a comprehensive policy aimed at fostering women entrepreneurship.
• The framework includes enhanced access to credit, mentorship programs, and market linkages for women-led businesses.
• Special focus on strengthening Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and integrating them into value chains.
• Initiatives to provide digital literacy and business management skills to women in rural and urban areas.
• The policy seeks to empower women economically and promote inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment.
prelimsPoints: Self-Help Groups (SHGs) are informal associations of people who choose to come together to find ways to improve their living conditions.
SHGs are crucial for financial inclusion and women's empowerment.
The Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) promotes SHGs.
Government schemes like 'Stand-Up India' support women entrepreneurs.
Gender Budgeting is a tool to assess the impact of government policies on women.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of women entrepreneurship and Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in promoting socio-economic development and gender equality in India.
Discuss the key challenges faced by women entrepreneurs in accessing finance, markets, and skills, and how the new policy addresses them.
Evaluate the role of government schemes and policy frameworks in fostering an enabling ecosystem for women-led businesses.
Examine the potential of digital literacy and technology adoption in empowering rural women entrepreneurs.
GS1.imageDescription: A group of women entrepreneurs showcasing their products, symbolizing economic empowerment.
GS1.trivia: India has over 12 million Self-Help Groups, with over 88% of them being all-women SHGs.

GS2.headline: International Day of Non-Violence Emphasizes Global Peace and Tolerance
GS2.explainer:
• The United Nations observes the International Day of Non-Violence on October 2nd, coinciding with Mahatma Gandhi's birth anniversary.
• UN Secretary-General issues a statement calling for renewed commitment to non-violence in resolving conflicts and promoting human rights.
• Global leaders and civil society organizations hold events to discuss the relevance of non-violent resistance in contemporary challenges.
• Focus on addressing hate speech, discrimination, and conflicts through peaceful means and dialogue.
• The day serves as a reminder of Gandhi's philosophy as a tool for social change and international peace.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: International Day of Non-Violence is observed annually on October 2nd.
It was established by the UN General Assembly in 2007.
The day promotes non-violence through education and public awareness.
It coincides with the birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of non-violent movements in achieving political and social change globally, with specific examples.
Analyze how Mahatma Gandhi's philosophy of non-violence remains relevant in addressing contemporary global challenges like terrorism and climate change.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international organizations like the UN in promoting peace and non-violence amidst geopolitical tensions.
Examine the challenges in implementing non-violent approaches to conflict resolution in an increasingly complex world.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations logo with a dove symbolizing peace.
GS2.trivia: The resolution to establish the International Day of Non-Violence was co-sponsored by 140 countries, reflecting its broad international support.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivers a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The Court clarifies provisions related to cross-border data transfers and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• Petitions challenging the Act on grounds of privacy infringement and excessive government access are partially dismissed.
• The ruling emphasizes the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests in data processing.
• This verdict provides crucial legal clarity for businesses and government agencies operating in the digital space.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was passed by Parliament in August 2023.
It aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
The Act recognizes the right to privacy as a fundamental right (Puttaswamy judgment).
It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
The Act defines 'data fiduciary' and 'data principal'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its implications for individual privacy and data governance in India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by the DPDP Act for businesses operating in the digital economy.
Evaluate the Supreme Court's role in balancing fundamental rights with state interests in the context of data protection.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how the Indian DPDP Act aligns or diverges from them.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, symbolizing a court judgment.
GS2.trivia: The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in 2017 in the K.S. Puttaswamy case.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold Annual Bilateral Summit, Focus on Economic & Strategic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• Prime Minister of India hosts his Japanese counterpart for the annual bilateral summit, reinforcing the 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
• Discussions center on enhancing economic ties, including investment, trade, and infrastructure development.
• Both leaders emphasize cooperation in defense, security, and clean energy technologies.
• Regional security issues, particularly in the Indo-Pacific, feature prominently in the talks.
• Joint statements highlight commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and collaboration in multilateral forums.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan hold annual bilateral summits.
Their relationship is designated as a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
Both countries are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue).
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects (e.g., High-Speed Rail).
Cooperation extends to defense, nuclear energy, and space.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of India-Japan relations into a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' and its significance for regional stability.
Discuss the key areas of cooperation between India and Japan, particularly in economic development, defense, and the Indo-Pacific strategy.
Evaluate the role of India and Japan within multilateral groupings like the Quad in addressing contemporary geopolitical challenges.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for further deepening bilateral ties, including trade imbalances and technological collaboration.
GS2.imageDescription: The Prime Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands, with their national flags in the background.
GS2.trivia: Japan is a major contributor to India's infrastructure development, notably through its Official Development Assistance (ODA), including for the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail Project.

GS2.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Comprehensive Report on Sustainable Rural Development Goals
GS2.explainer:
• NITI Aayog publishes its annual report assessing India's progress towards achieving Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in rural areas.
• The report highlights successes in poverty reduction, access to clean water, and rural electrification.
• Identifies persistent challenges in quality education, healthcare infrastructure, and gender equality in remote regions.
• Recommends policy interventions focusing on localized planning, community participation, and digital inclusion.
• The findings will inform future government strategies for holistic rural development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
keyTerms: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.
It replaced the Planning Commission in 2015.
NITI Aayog prepares long-term policy and program frameworks.
The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a collection of 17 global goals set by the United Nations.
India is a signatory to the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of NITI Aayog in shaping India's rural development policies and monitoring progress on Sustainable Development Goals.
Discuss the major achievements and persistent challenges in achieving inclusive and sustainable rural development in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes aimed at poverty reduction, improving rural infrastructure, and enhancing access to basic services.
Examine the importance of localized planning and community participation in ensuring the success of rural development initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic representing the Sustainable Development Goals with rural Indian landscapes.
GS2.trivia: NITI Aayog's full form is National Institution for Transforming India.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Vocational Training and Multidisciplinary Learning in 2025
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announces new directives for the phased implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in higher education.
• Emphasis on integrating vocational training and skill development into mainstream curricula across universities and colleges.
• Push for multidisciplinary and holistic education, breaking down rigid disciplinary silos.
• Initiatives to enhance faculty training and adopt technology-driven learning platforms.
• The goal is to produce a workforce equipped with 21st-century skills and foster critical thinking.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the NEP of 1986.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Key features include 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, multidisciplinary approach, vocational integration.
The Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) allows credit transfer.
NEP 2020 aims to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in reshaping India's education landscape.
Discuss the challenges in implementing vocational training and multidisciplinary learning across diverse educational institutions in India.
Evaluate how NEP 2020 aims to address issues of quality, access, and equity in higher education.
Examine the role of technology and faculty development in achieving the goals of the National Education Policy.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in a vocational training workshop, symbolizing skill development.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Digital Health Ecosystem
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) releases a progress report on the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), highlighting its expanding reach.
• Over 500 million health IDs (ABHA IDs) registered, facilitating digital health records and seamless access to healthcare services.
• Focus on integrating private healthcare providers and expanding the network of Health Facility Registry (HFR) and Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR).
• Challenges remain in ensuring digital literacy and privacy concerns in remote and underserved areas.
• ABDM aims to create a unified digital health infrastructure for efficient and equitable healthcare delivery.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) was launched in September 2021.
It aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country.
Key components include Health ID (ABHA), Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR), and Health Facility Registry (HFR).
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
ABDM facilitates digital access to health records and telemedicine services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to transform India's healthcare delivery system.
Discuss the challenges in ensuring data privacy, digital literacy, and equitable access to digital health services across India.
Evaluate the role of technology and public-private partnerships in expanding the reach and effectiveness of ABDM.
Examine how ABDM aligns with India's broader vision of 'Digital India' and universal healthcare coverage.
GS2.imageDescription: A digital graphic depicting interconnected healthcare services and an ABHA ID card.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

GS2.headline: India Pushes for G20 Consensus on Sustainable Infrastructure Financing
GS2.explainer:
• India, as a key player in the G20, actively advocates for stronger international cooperation on sustainable infrastructure financing.
• Discussions at a G20 working group meeting emphasize innovative financing mechanisms and private sector participation.
• Focus on green infrastructure projects, climate-resilient development, and digital connectivity in developing nations.
• India highlights its own experience with large-scale infrastructure development and public-private partnerships.
• The initiative aims to bridge the global infrastructure gap and promote inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
It addresses major issues related to the global economy.
India held the G20 presidency in 2023.
Sustainable infrastructure is a key focus area for global development.
Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) play a crucial role in infrastructure financing.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and objectives in advocating for sustainable infrastructure financing within the G20 framework.
Discuss the importance of green infrastructure and climate-resilient development for achieving global sustainable development goals.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in mobilizing private sector investment for large-scale infrastructure projects in developing countries.
Examine how innovative financing mechanisms can help bridge the global infrastructure gap and promote inclusive growth.
GS2.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green elements, symbolizing sustainable infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: The G20 accounts for around 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

GS2.headline: India-Central Asia Dialogue Focuses on Connectivity, Trade, and Regional Security
GS2.explainer:
• The latest edition of the India-Central Asia Dialogue concludes with renewed pledges for enhanced cooperation across various sectors.
• Foreign Ministers discuss increasing trade volumes, establishing reliable connectivity corridors, and energy security.
• Emphasis on regional security challenges, including the situation in Afghanistan and countering terrorism.
• India reiterates its commitment to the 'Connect Central Asia' policy and strengthening historical and cultural ties.
• Joint statement calls for greater collaboration in multilateral forums and people-to-people exchanges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The India-Central Asia Dialogue is a ministerial-level forum.
It involves India and five Central Asian countries: Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan.
India's 'Connect Central Asia Policy' was launched in 2012.
Key areas of cooperation include trade, connectivity, energy, and security.
Chabahar Port in Iran is crucial for India's connectivity to Central Asia.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing connectivity and trade between India and Central Asian countries.
Evaluate India's role in addressing regional security concerns, particularly the situation in Afghanistan, through the India-Central Asia Dialogue.
Examine how historical and cultural linkages can be leveraged to strengthen people-to-people ties between India and Central Asia.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing trade routes connecting India to Central Asian countries.
GS2.trivia: Central Asia is rich in natural resources, including oil, natural gas, and minerals, making it strategically important.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties and Domestic Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concludes its bi-monthly review, opting to keep the repo rate unchanged.
• Decision cited global economic slowdown, geopolitical tensions, and persistent domestic food inflation as key factors.
• RBI Governor reiterates commitment to inflation targeting while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future rate actions contingent on inflation trajectory and growth momentum.
• The move aims to provide stability to financial markets and anchor inflation expectations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy and fiscal policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body (3 RBI, 3 GoI nominees).
The MPC determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target for India is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global and domestic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
Evaluate the impact of global geopolitical events and supply chain disruptions on India's inflation trajectory and RBI's policy decisions.
Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in ensuring transparency and accountability in India's monetary policy framework.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Climate Targets, Highlights Progress in Renewable Energy
GS3.explainer:
• On the sidelines of global climate discussions, India reiterates its ambitious Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change releases a report detailing advancements in renewable energy capacity, particularly solar and wind power.
• Challenges remain in financing adaptation measures and transitioning away from fossil fuels.
• India calls for greater climate finance and technology transfer from developed nations.
• Focus on sustainable development goals and green hydrogen initiatives to meet future energy demands.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India updated its NDCs in 2022, committing to reduce emissions intensity by 45% by 2030 from 2005 levels.
India aims for about 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030.
The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India.
Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2 degrees Celsius.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's progress and challenges in achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
Discuss the potential of renewable energy, particularly green hydrogen, in decarbonizing India's economy and ensuring energy security.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and climate finance in enabling developing countries like India to meet their climate goals.
Examine the environmental and socio-economic impacts of India's transition towards a low-carbon economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels generating electricity in a field, symbolizing renewable energy.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases globally, but its per capita emissions are significantly lower than many developed nations.

GS3.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Chandrayaan-4 Mission Preparations for Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) releases a statement on the advanced stages of preparation for the Chandrayaan-4 mission.
• The mission aims for a lunar sample return, building on the success of Chandrayaan-3's soft landing.
• Focus on developing advanced robotic systems and sample collection mechanisms for autonomous operation on the lunar surface.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a crucial step towards future human spaceflight missions and deeper lunar exploration.
• The mission highlights India's growing capabilities in complex interplanetary missions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-1 was India's first lunar probe (2008).
Chandrayaan-2 attempted a soft landing (2019) but the lander crashed.
Chandrayaan-3 successfully soft-landed a lander (Vikram) and rover (Pragyan) on the lunar south pole in August 2023.
Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a lunar sample return mission.
ISRO is the national space agency of India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's lunar missions (Chandrayaan series) for scientific research and technological advancement.
Analyze the challenges involved in a lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the role of India's space program in fostering scientific temper and inspiring innovation among youth.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its potential for international collaboration.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Chandrayaan-4 spacecraft orbiting the Moon.
GS3.trivia: India became the fourth country to achieve a soft landing on the Moon and the first to land near the lunar south pole with Chandrayaan-3.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Indigenous Defense Manufacturing with New Policy Incentives
GS3.explainer:
• Ministry of Defence announces a fresh set of policy reforms and incentives to promote 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defense production.
• Focus on encouraging private sector participation, MSMEs, and startups in developing cutting-edge defense technologies.
• Measures include streamlined procurement procedures, increased budget allocation for domestic R&D, and technology transfer facilitation.
• The goal is to reduce import dependency and position India as a global defense manufacturing hub.
• This move is crucial for national security and economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan includes a focus on self-reliance in defense production.
The Defense Acquisition Procedure (DAP) promotes 'Make in India'.
India aims to become a net exporter of defense equipment.
Defense Industrial Corridors have been established in Uttar Pradesh and Tamil Nadu.
Positive Indigenisation Lists restrict import of certain defense items.
